title: Magdelyn Herzig
text:
Hi! I’m Magdelyn. I grew up in rural Southern Oregon and moved to the Twin Cities for college at St. Catherine University, where I studied geography and data science. I did research on the modern impacts of racial covenants on schooling and public health, the impacts of dam removal on long term vegetative health using remotely sensed imagery, as well as the demonstrated cultural resilience and mobility among pastoral reindeer herders in Sápmi.
Professionally, as a Research Intern at the Metropolitan Council, I have been involved in multiple projects, involving mapping and analyzing the regional parks and trails system, and using deep learning methodologies to process high resolution aerial imagery to delineate urban tree canopies. I believe qualitative and quantitative methods and practices are stronger together than apart, and that data must be connected to people and the human experience to be meaningful.
This portfolio was created to organize and synthesize my personal work and research interests, including remote sensing, natural language processing, deep learning, computer vision, equity analyses, cartography and data visualization. I currently live in Minneapolis, Minnesota with two cats and a sourdough starter. I’m an avid library card user, baker, and amateur accordion player.

title: Hi! I’m Magdelyn Herzig.
text: I graduated from St. Catherine University with a B.A. in Geography and a B.S. in Data Science in December 2025. I am currently a Research Intern at the Metropolitan Council of the Twin Cities, working on a project using deep learning methods to delineate urban tree canopy. My interests include remote sensing, equity analyses, deep learning, computer vision, natural language processing, and qualitative methodologies.
